signals.title: GBP/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Inflation Divergence
signals.text: GBP/USD downside pressure intensifies as hotter-than-expected US CPI and PPI data drive hawkish Fed bets against a weakening GBP. The pair faces a -17bp OIS carry disadvantage (SOFR 3.56% vs SONIA 3.73%) which is failing to support the Pound as US 10Y yields surge to 4.595%. GBP COT net long +40,980 (70% long) creates significant liquidation risk if the 1.3300 support level fails at the open.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Commodity Correction + Retail Long Squeeze
signals.text: NZD/USD downside bias is reinforced by a sharp retreat in soybean prices and broader commodity weakness following Friday's US inflation roar. Extreme retail sentiment (87% long) diverges from the NZD COT net short -19,437, signaling a high probability of a stop-run toward 0.5800. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 27 May is the primary catalyst for determining if the current hike bias can offset the 150bp USD carry advantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind + Yield Gap
signals.text: AUD COT net long +55,794 (74.8% long) creates a crowded-long unwind risk as the pair closed Friday at 0.7153 amid a global equity sell-off. While the RBA maintains a 4.35% rate with a hike bias, the 79bp OIS carry advantage for AUD is being neutralized by the 4.595% US 10Y yield surge. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 16% hike priced) on 16 June determines if the policy divergence can sustain the bid against a bear-steepening US curve.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Advantage + Options Downside Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY upside momentum is supported by a 283bp OIS carry advantage and a JPY COT net short of -70,605, though a strong options put skew (RR -1.73) signals hedging against a sudden reversal. The 158.73 Friday close sits near multi-week highs as US inflation risks push bond yields higher, increasing the cost of JPY shorts. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 16 June is the catalyst for any potential policy shift to address the widening yield gap.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Technical Breakdown + Carry Disadvantage
signals.text: EUR/USD downside targets 1.1600 after breaking below a major technical support cluster as sellers lean against corrective resistance. The 163bp OIS carry advantage for USD (SOFR 3.56% vs ESTR 1.93%) remains a structural headwind for the Euro. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 June will resolve whether the current hold bias can withstand the widening US-German 10Y yield spread of 149.5bp.
